message:
🌱 Add hami: CUDA_DISABLE_CONTROL=true has no effect — HAMI still mission (#800)

Signed-off-by: Andy Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,77 @@
+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "hami-1050-cuda-disable-control-true-has-no-effect-hami-still-hijacks-gpu-behavio",
+  "missionClass": "solution",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "hami: CUDA_DISABLE_CONTROL=true has no effect — HAMI still hijacks GPU behavior",
+    "description": "CUDA_DISABLE_CONTROL=true has no effect — HAMI still hijacks GPU behavior. Community-requested feature.",
+    "type": "feature",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Check current hami deployment",
+        "description": "Verify your hami version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n hami -l app.kubernetes.io/name=hami
helm list -n hami 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working hami installation."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Review hami configuration",
+        "description": "Inspect the relevant hami configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n hami -l app.kubernetes.io/name=hami
kubectl get configmap -n hami -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=hami
```
**Description:**

Hi,

I’m currently running a container with the following setup:

* GPU: NVIDIA L40S
* Driver version: 570.124.06
* CUDA version: 12.8

Even when I set `CUDA_DISABLE_CONTROL=true`, I’m seeing that `hami` still hijacks control of"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Apply the fix for CUDA_DISABLE_CONTROL=true has no effect — HAMI still…",
+        "description": "Add a new test case that old code can't pass and the new code can pass.
```yaml
[HAMI-core Msg]: Initializing.....
[HAMI-core Msg]: Calling exit handler
```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Verify the feature works",
+        "description": "Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n hami -l app.kubernetes.io/name=hami
kubectl get events -n hami --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "CUDA_DISABLE_CONTROL=true has no effect — HAMI still…" is functioning correctly."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "Add a new test case that old code can't pass and the new code can pass.",
+      "codeSnippets": [
+        "[HAMI-core Msg]: Initializing.....
[HAMI-core Msg]: Calling exit handler",
+        "apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: model
spec:
  template:
    spec:
      containers:
      - args:
        - sleep
        - infinity
        env:
        - name: CUDA_DISABLE_CONTROL
          value: "true"
        image: tritonserver:25.04-trtllm-python-py3
        name: triton
        resources:
          limits:
            nvidia.com/gpu: "2"
          requests:
            nvidia.com/gpu: "2"
        volumeMounts:
        - mountPath: /etc/ld.so.preload
          name: hami-preload-blocker
          readOnly: true
          subPath: preload
      initContainers:
      - command:
        - sh
        - -c
        - echo '' > /preload/preload
        image: busybox
        name: hami-preload-blocker
        volumeMounts:
        - mountPath: /preload
          nam",
+        "apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  name: cuda-gpu-pod
spec:
  tolerations:
    - effect: NoSchedule
      key: nvidia.com/gpu
      operator: Exists
  containers:
    - name: busybox
      image: busybox
      command: ["sleep", "infinity"]
    - name: cuda
      image: nvidia/cuda:12.4.1-cudnn-devel-ubuntu22.04
      command: ["/bin/sleep", "infinity"]
      env:
        - name: CUDA_DISABLE_CONTROL
          value: "true"
      resources:
        limits:
          nvidia.com/gpu: "1""
+      ]
+    }
+  },
